reaches.toDesc: U.S. Geological Survey gauging station cable crossing at mouth of Antelope Creek Canyon

reaches.toDesc: mouth of Antelope Creek (confluence with the Sacramento River)

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 256270, "Antelope Creek", Stream, CA, Tehama Co.
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":256270,"gaz_name":"Antelope Creek","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Tehama","objectid_1":1391073}. Corrects prior file coordinates (-122.0000, 40.1550), which appear to have been a rounded/guessed estimate rather than a GNIS pull; the corrected point (-122.1342, 40.1618), near 40°09'42"N 122°08'03"W, is corroborated by the Wikipedia infobox coordinate for Antelope Creek (Tehama County) and by Wikidata Q28873753 (40°9'55"N, 122°7'48"W). Note a distinct, unrelated 'Antelope Creek' also exists in Tehama Co. (gaz_id 218298, a 7-mile tributary of Cottonwood Creek near 40.374,-122.437) which must not be confused with this Sacramento River tributary.

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.40(b)(5) — Antelope Creek (Tehama Co.)
sources.quotedText: (5) Antelope Creek (Tehama Co.). (A) From confluence with North Fork downstream to U.S. Geological Survey gauging station cable crossing at mouth of Antelope Creek Canyon. Last Sat. in Apr. through Nov. 15. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession. (B) From U.S. Geological Survey gauging station cable crossing at mouth of Antelope Creek Canyon downstream to mouth of Antelope Creek. Jun. 16 through Sep. 30. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.40 — Hatchery Trout/Steelhead Footnote (**) Definition
sources.quotedText: Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

groups.humanSummary: Antelope Creek from the confluence with the North Fork downstream to the USGS gauging station cable crossing at the mouth of Antelope Creek Canyon (Tehama Co.): open the last Saturday in April through November 15, artificial lures with barbless hooks only, 2 hatchery trout/steelhead daily (4 in possession).
groups.verbatimText: (A) From confluence with North Fork downstream to U.S. Geological Survey gauging station cable crossing at mouth of Antelope Creek Canyon. Last Sat. in Apr. through Nov. 15. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

groups.humanSummary: Antelope Creek from the USGS gauging station cable crossing at the mouth of Antelope Creek Canyon downstream to the mouth of Antelope Creek: open June 16 through September 30, 2 hatchery trout/steelhead daily (4 in possession).
groups.verbatimText: (B) From U.S. Geological Survey gauging station cable crossing at mouth of Antelope Creek Canyon downstream to mouth of Antelope Creek. Jun. 16 through Sep. 30. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

regulations.humanSummary: Open the last Saturday in April through November 15, North Fork confluence to canyon-mouth gauging station.
regulations.verbatimText: Last Sat. in Apr. through Nov. 15.

parameters.note: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used, North Fork confluence to canyon-mouth gauging station.
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.counting_rule: Combined limit of hatchery trout and/or hatchery steelhead (fish showing a healed adipose fin clip); 2 fish daily, 4 fish in possession.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily, 4 in possession, North Fork confluence to canyon-mouth gauging station.
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

regulations.humanSummary: Open June 16 through September 30, canyon-mouth gauging station to the mouth of Antelope Creek.

parameters.counting_rule: Combined limit of hatchery trout and/or hatchery steelhead (fish showing a healed adipose fin clip); 2 fish daily, 4 fish in possession.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily, 4 in possession, canyon-mouth gauging station to the mouth of Antelope Creek.
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

parameters.condition: Trout or steelhead lacking a healed adipose fin clip (i.e. wild/unmarked fish)
parameters.note: Only hatchery-marked fish (healed adipose fin clip) may be retained; all other trout and steelhead must be released immediately unharmed.

regulations.humanSummary: Only adipose-fin-clipped hatchery trout/steelhead may be kept; unmarked wild fish must be released immediately.
regulations.verbatimText: Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
